known_blockers: Restricted-source payload ingestion remains gated to source-specific tracks and local-only policies.
Per-source authoritative release versions are not pinned until each ontology integration track performs source-specific access and license review.
Remote PR, GitHub Actions, and merge evidence is not recorded until this local implementation is committed and opened upstream.
expected_blockers: Different ontology licenses and access modes will limit which network artifacts are PR-safe or release-safe.
Cross-ontology mappings will create one-to-many, many-to-one, deprecated, circular, and language-specific conflicts.
Reviewer capacity may become the bottleneck once conflict heatmaps and comparison packs are generated.
fallback_path: Deliver registry schema, source access matrix, artifact contracts, and conflict-report interfaces even if restricted source payloads remain local-only.

restricted_payload_policy.commit_policy: network artifacts may commit registry schemas, source access matrix, provenance, validation, conflict, and coverage reports only when they do not reconstruct restricted source payloads; restricted source payloads stay local-only
required_evidence: local-only manifest if any restricted source is touched
redaction or exclusion proof before commit

fail_fast_probe.scope: one open source record, one restricted-source governance-only record, one crosswalk edge, one conflict row, and one review-pack row
success_criteria: restricted rows do not leak payload text

evidence_score_policy.promotion_rule: no ontology or LLM signal promotes a translation without human review
